subtopics.title: Structure and Functions of Nigerian Government
common_misconceptions: Nigeria has a parliamentary system (presidential system)
The President is above the law (not under 1999 Constitution)
State governments have no powers (they have significant powers under federalism)
subtopics.teaching_note: Nigerian government structure: Executive (President, Vice President, Federal Executive Council/Cabinet, Ministries). Legislature (National Assembly: Senate - 109 members, House of Representatives - 360 members). Judiciary (Supreme Court, Court of Appeal, Federal High Court, State High Courts, Sharia Court of Appeal, Customary Court of Appeal). Federal character: principle of proportional representation. Creation of states: 3 regions (1960) → 4 (1963) → 12 (1967) → 19 (1976) → 21 (1987) → 30 (1991) → 36 (1996) + FCT. Revenue allocation: derivation principle, need principle, population, equality of states.
